contentBuildFallbackNotice.message: Links point to the default branch because documentation for the release tag is unavailable.

contentFormatHintDeb.message: Package for Debian, Ubuntu and derivatives

contentFormatHintRpm.message: Package for Fedora, RHEL and derivatives

contentInstallAfterDownload.message: The file is downloading — what to do next

contentMoreOnRelease.message: $COUNT$ more files on the release page

contentNoAssets.message: The release has no uploaded binaries. You can download its source code.

contentNoRelease.message: This repository has no suitable published release.

contentRateLimitedUntil.message: GitHub API rate limit reached. Try again after $TIME$.

contentStaleText.message: It was published $DATE$. Check whether the project is still maintained before downloading.

contentWatchText.message: The extension will check new GitHub Releases locally.

extensionDescription.message: Adds a smart GitHub release download button, remembers downloads, and optionally watches repositories for new releases.

guideAabSummary.message: An AAB is intended for publishing and build tools, not direct installation on a phone.

guideApkSummary.message: Open the file on Android. The system may ask you to allow installs from the source app.

guideApkWarning.message: Installing apps outside Google Play increases malware risk.

guideApksSummary.message: This is a split-APK bundle and cannot be installed like a normal APK. A compatible split-APK installer is required.

guideApksTitle.message: How to install the APKS bundle

guideAppimageSummary.message: An AppImage does not need installation, but the file must be executable.

guideArchiveSummary.message: This is an archive, not a universal installer. Read the project documentation after extracting it.

guideCheckAppSource.message: Check the app name, permissions and source

guideCheckDeveloper.message: Check the developer name before confirming

guideCheckWindowsPublisher.message: Check the project and publisher shown by Windows

guideCrxSummary.message: Chrome normally blocks direct CRX installation from arbitrary sites. Use the official store or an unpacked build in developer mode.

guideCrxWarning.message: Do not disable browser protections for an unknown CRX

guideDebSummary.message: This command is suitable for Debian, Ubuntu, Linux Mint and most derivatives.

guideDebTitle.message: How to install the DEB package

guideDmgSummary.message: Open the disk image. Usually, drag the application into the Applications folder.

guideDmgTitle.message: How to install from the DMG

guideEjectDmg.message: Eject the disk image in Finder after installation

guideExeSummary.message: Open the downloaded file and follow the installer. Portable builds may not require installation.

guideExplorerExtractAll.message: In File Explorer, choose Extract all

guideFindApk.message: For normal installation, look for an APK or APKS asset in this release

guideInstallLocalPackage.message: Install the local package and its dependencies

guideInstallTrustedFile.message: Install only a file from a publisher you trust

guideInstallVsix.message: Install with the VS Code command line

guideJarSummary.message: A compatible Java Runtime Environment must be installed.

guideMsiSummary.message: Normally, double-clicking the file is sufficient.

guideMsiTitle.message: How to install the MSI package

guidePkgSummary.message: Double-click the package and follow the macOS installer prompts.

guideReadAfterExtract.message: Read README or INSTALL after extracting it.

guideRpmFedora.description: RPM installation option for Fedora-family distributions.

guideRpmOpenSuse.description: RPM installation option for openSUSE-family distributions.

guideRpmSummary.message: Choose the command for your distribution.

guideRpmTitle.message: How to install the RPM package

guideRunFromDownloads.message: Run it from the download folder

guideRunSummary.message: Review the file first and make sure you trust the project.

guideRunTrustedOnly.message: Run files only from a project you trust

guideScriptWarning.message: A script can change files and system settings. Do not add sudo unless the project explicitly requires it.

guideSnapSummary.message: Local Snap files from GitHub usually do not include a Snap Store assertion.

guideSnapTitle.message: How to install the local Snap

guideSnapWarning.message: The --dangerous option disables Snap Store assertion and signature validation.

guideTarBz2Title.message: How to extract the TAR.BZ2 archive

guideTarGzTitle.message: How to extract the TAR.GZ archive

guideTarXzTitle.message: How to extract the TAR.XZ archive

guideTarZstTitle.message: How to extract the TAR.ZST archive

guideTrustedExtensions.message: Install extensions only from trusted sources

guideUseTrustedInstaller.message: Check the project documentation and use a trusted installer

guideVsixSummary.message: The package is intended for Visual Studio Code and compatible editors.

guideWindowsPackageSummary.message: Open the file in App Installer and check the publisher details before installing.

guideWindowsPackageTitle.message: How to install the Windows package

guideXpiSummary.message: Open the file in Firefox and confirm the add-on installation.

guideZipSummary.message: After extracting, read README or INSTALL inside the archive.

guideZipTitle.message: How to extract the ZIP archive

guideZipWindowsSummary.message: Extract the archive, then look for an EXE file or README.

guideZipWindowsTitle.message: How to use the ZIP archive

optionsAuthBenefitDiscovery.message: More reliable discovery of instructions in linked READMEs and checks for watched repositories.

optionsAuthBenefitLimit.message: Up to 5,000 API requests per hour instead of the shared anonymous limit.

optionsAuthBenefitPrivacy.message: No password or cookies. Private repositories are not read; the token stays local and is not synced. Disconnect removes the local copy.

optionsAuthConsentDenied.message: GitHub cannot be connected without permission to transmit authentication information.

optionsAuthError.message: GitHub could not be connected. Please try again.

optionsAuthExpired.message: The one-time code expired. Start the connection again.

optionsAuthNote.message: Optional. Official GitHub authorization raises the API limit and makes linked-instruction discovery and update checks more reliable.

optionsAuthPendingNote.message: Enter the code on the official GitHub page. The extension will detect approval automatically.

optionsAuthRemoved.message: The GitHub connection was removed from this device.

optionsGuidanceBeginner.message: Show after download and in the menu

optionsGuidanceCompact.message: Only on request in the menu

optionsHistoryEnabled.message: Record downloads made through the extension

optionsMonthsFew.description: Localized month count for the few plural category.

optionsMonthsMany.description: Localized month count for the many plural category.

optionsMonthsOne.description: Localized month count for the one plural category.

optionsMonthsOther.description: Localized month count for the other plural category.

optionsStale.message: Warn when release is older than

optionsSubtitle.message: Configure recommendations, interface and background update checks.

optionsSystemNote.message: Keep automatic detection unless the extension identifies your device incorrectly.

optionsUpdatesNote.message: Download history and watched repositories are stored locally on this device.

popupHistoryNote.message: Only downloads started through this extension.

popupNoTracking.message: Allow tracking after a download and the project will appear here.

popupTrackingNote.message: Only repositories you explicitly watch are checked.
